response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS web applications for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work (DLang microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching at Sociomantic). However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and his C++ exposure is limited and dated (NI LabWindows components, a thesis project), with his recent decade centered on TypeScript/Node full-stack work. He has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation-guidance pipeline experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map GIS platforms
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers in DLang)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, Linux proficiency
concerns: Modern C++ (or Rust) experience is minimal and not recent; primary stack is TypeScript/Node
No direct experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance, or map-data pipelines at production scale
Recent roles are web/product full-stack, distant from distributed data-pipeline operations
